channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Government asks that detained Columbia student's legal fight be moved to New Jersey or Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A government lawyer asked a federal judge in Manhattan on Wednesday to move the legal fight over the detention of Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil to New Jersey or Louisiana, where he's being held as the Trump administration seeks to deport him over his participati
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina House Republicans fight with each other over the budget
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — At least three-quarters of South Carolina's House members approved the more than 100 sections of the state's $14 billion spending plan this week, but is wasn't as easy as that makes it seem.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In-N-Out CEO Lynsi Snyder says her company remains a family despite ‘betrayal’ from colleagues
item.description: Lynsi Snyder inherited the title of CEO of In-N-Out Burger, and as the only living member of the founding family, she continues to run the ever-growing business like her own family.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump campaigned as a protector of free speech. Critics say his actions as president threaten it
item.description: DENVER (AP) — When President Donald Trump gave his joint address to Congress last week, he boasted that in his first few weeks back in the White House he had “brought free speech back to America.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police reconnect father and daughter who was kidnapped decades ago
item.description: Police are announcing they've re-connected a father and his daughter, who was kidnapped from New Haven decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration halts $1 billion program that keeps affordable housing livable
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American student, 20, vanishes in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: Authorities are continuing efforts to find Sudiksha Konanki, a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student who has been missing for nearly a week while on a spring break trip in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KTLA 5

item.title: Ex-Tennessee lawmaker announces Trump's pardon 2 weeks into prison time for campaign finance scheme
item.description: A former Republican Tennessee lawmaker says President Donald Trump has pardoned him two weeks into his 21-month prison sentence for an illegal campaign finance scheme.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Risks of state abortion reporting mandates outweigh the benefits, an advocacy group says
item.description: States should stop requiring health providers to file reports on every abortion because the information poses a risk to both them and their patients in the current political environment, a research group that advocates for abortion access says.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Study shows rain-soaking atmospheric rivers are getting bigger, wetter and more frequent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As extreme weather events have hit the world hard in recent years, one meteorology term — atmospheric rivers — has made the leap from scientific circles to common language, particularly in places that have been hit by them. That stands to reason.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EU retaliates as Trump steel, aluminum tariffs take effect
item.description: After announcing the response, the EU released a 99-page list of possible items that could be subject to tariffs, including fruit and vegetables, meat, alcoholic beverages, nicotine vapes and chewing gum.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis marks important milestones this week as he recovers from pneumonia
item.description: The recovery of Pope Francis from double pneumonia continued Wednesday as he inched toward notching some important milestones in his papacy: The 12th anniversary Thursday of his election as pope and a near-record in hospital stays for a pontiff.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education secretary: Mass layoffs first step toward total shutdown
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon affirmed late Tuesday that mass layoffs are the first step toward shuttering the Education Department — a long-time goal of President Donald Trump's since his time on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: The US is short nearly 4 million homes
item.description: Builders faced barriers like zoning and supply costs, which make producing affordable homes challenging.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beth Holloway identifies key difference between Sudiksha Konanki case and her daughter's
item.description: The mother of an American teenager who was killed while vacationing in Aruba said the search for the 20-year-old college student who went missing in the Dominican Republic has one important distinction
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US bans flights to Haiti's capital until Sept. 8 as UN expert says gang violence is more dire
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Tuesday extended its ban on flights to Haiti’s capital until Sept. 8 because of escalating gang violence, which the U.N.’s human rights expert on the Caribbean nation said is more dire than ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canada electricity tariffs to target 3 US states
item.description: "If the United States escalates, I will not hesitate to shut the electricity off completely," said Doug Ford.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Medical helicopter crash in Mississippi kills 3
item.description: MADISON COUNTY, Miss. (AP) — A medical transport helicopter crashed in Mississippi on Monday, killing a pilot and two hospital workers on board, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities, including Northwestern University, under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: LA district attorney says he won’t support resentencing the Menendez brothers because they lied
item.description: Resentencing, which could make the brothers immediately eligible for parole, is just one of the pathways they are pursuing to walk free after their 1996 convictions for murder.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Juarez police officer injured in body shop attack that left 3 dead
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Three men were killed and two injured by gunfire during a shootout Sunday at a mechanical shop in Juarez, Mexico. Juarez police said at least two men arrived in a vehicle at the business in the Heroes de la Revolucion neighborhood around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Stroller accessory recalled after 6 children suffer finger lacerations: CPSC
item.description: Consumers who may have purchased or installed the accessories are being instructed to immediately discontinue use.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, Fox 5

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: President Trump defends tariff policy
item.description: President Donald Trump continues to defend tariffs as a way to address unfair trade and illegal drugs coming across the border, but critics warn they'll cause higher prices for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WGN 9

item.title: Measles case confirmed in Maryland resident who recently travelled abroad
item.description: Early symptoms include a fever higher than 101 degrees, a runny nose, cough and red, watery eyes. A person will experience a red rash one to four days after early symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Search continues for University of Pittsburgh student missing in Punta Cana
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA)— Authorities in the Dominican Republic are searching for a University of Pittsburgh student who went missing in Punta Cana on March 6.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Immigration agents arrest Palestinian activist who helped lead Columbia University protests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal immigration authorities arrested a Palestinian activist Saturday who played a prominent role in Columbia University’s protests against Israel, a significant escalation in the Trump administration’s pledge to detain and deport student activists.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Trump isn't alone in his geopolitical aspirations. Some in Illinois and Oregon want change too
item.description: As President Donald Trump floats grand ideas like reacquiring the Panama Canal and taking over Greenland, some rural residents of Illinois and Oregon are promoting geopolitical change of their own: They want to break away from their states, and perhaps unite with Indiana and Idaho.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Warriors star Stephen Curry reaches 25,000 career points
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Stephen Curry became the 26th player in NBA history to score 25,000 career points, reaching the milestone during the third quarter of the Golden State Warriors' 115-110 victory over the Detroit Pistons on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5
